metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify how the sdist-test job achieves a non-null COMMIT in the wheel it builds from the sdist — either document that the job also sets `REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT=${GITHUB_SHA::7}` (mirroring the build job), or clarify that the sdist-test job installs the *pre-built wheel* extracted from the sdist artifact rather than rebuilding a wheel, so no fresh hook invocation occurs.
findings.finding: The sdist-test job's 'non-null COMMIT assert' is unmeetable as described: the What section's hook behavior (env var unset → git fallback; no failure when unset) means the sdist-test job — which has no .git checkout and no described mechanism to set REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT — would silently produce COMMIT = None, directly contradicting AC (b)'s requirement for a non-null COMMIT assertion from the sdist-test path.
findings.location: AC (b) vs. 'What' section — 'Fix the hook' and 'Fail-fast scoped to the release context' subsections vs. 'Test the exact sdist' bullet
findings.suggested_fix: In the 'Test the exact sdist' What bullet, add: 'the sdist-test job sets REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT=${GITHUB_SHA::7} before invoking pip/build so the hook's env-var path is exercised and COMMIT is non-null.' Alternatively, reframe the sdist-test as installing a pre-built wheel from the sdist tarball (pip installs sdists by building a wheel internally, so the env var is still needed — the simplest resolution is to also set REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT in the sdist-test job and document this in the plan).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify explicitly whether the sdist-test job sets `REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT` before installing/building from the sdist (required for the non-null COMMIT assert to pass), or clarify that the non-null assert in AC #2 applies only to the wheel-test job where the env var is inherited from the build job.
findings.finding: The sdist-test job's 'non-null COMMIT assert' is structurally underspecified: the hook's behavior when `REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT` is **unset** is a graceful git fallback (no failure), but the sdist-test job installs from a downloaded sdist tarball with no `.git` directory present and no `REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT` env var set (the plan only says the *build* job sets it). In that environment, the git fallback yields `COMMIT = None`, making the non-null assert unmeetable unless the sdist-test job also sets `REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT`. The plan does not specify whether the sdist-test job inherits or re-sets the env var.
findings.location: What section — 'Test the exact sdist' bullet; AC #2
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the 'Test the exact sdist' bullet: 'the sdist-test job sets `REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT` to the same short SHA (passed as a job output from the build job) before running `pip install <sdist>` so the rebuilt wheel carries the real SHA and the non-null assert passes.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate `github_release` as a consumer of the restructured artifact bundle in the Scope section, and explicitly state whether SHA256SUMS (and any renamed bundle) should be included in or excluded from the GitHub Release upload.
findings.finding: The `github_release` job in `release.yml` (lines 75–107) downloads the artifact bundle with `name: dist` and uploads `dist/*` directly to the GitHub Release via `gh release upload "$TAG" dist/*`. The plan restructures this bundle — adding at minimum a `SHA256SUMS` file — but does not enumerate `github_release` as a consumer requiring review. After the plan lands, SHA256SUMS (and potentially renamed bundle contents) will be attached to every GitHub Release without the plan acknowledging this behavior.
findings.location: Scope section / release.yml lines 75–107 (github_release job)
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Scope: 'github_release job — update `name:` in download-artifact step if bundle is renamed; decide whether SHA256SUMS should be attached to the GitHub Release (recommend yes: it gives users a hash-verification path).' Or add a `--exclude '*.txt'` glob to the gh release upload to keep the release clean.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate the `github_release` job in the Scope section and explicitly state whether `SHA256SUMS` should be included in or excluded from the GitHub Release assets (e.g., confirm the `dist/*` glob intentionally includes it as a provenance artifact, or update the glob to `dist/*.whl dist/*.tar.gz`).
findings.finding: The plan's Scope section omits the `github_release` job as a consumer of the restructured artifact bundle. After this plan adds a `SHA256SUMS` file to the bundle (AC1), the existing `github_release` job's `gh release upload "$TAG" dist/* --clobber` glob will silently include `SHA256SUMS` as a GitHub Release asset, since the job downloads `name: dist` and uploads all of `dist/*`. The plan never states whether this inclusion is intentional (it may be desirable as provenance) or whether the glob should be narrowed — forcing the executor to make an undocumented decision.
findings.location: Scope section / .github/workflows/release.yml github_release job (lines 75-107) / AC1

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the exact line format of the `evidence-artifacts` fragment (e.g., GNU `sha256sum` format: `<hex>  <filename>` per line) in the What section or as a new Scope item, so this story and F4's consolidation step share a concrete schema.
findings.finding: The `evidence-artifacts` fragment that the build-once job must upload (AC #4, What section) has no defined format/schema — the plan does not specify what fields, line format, or key names the fragment contains, yet F4's consolidation step must concatenate it verbatim into `release-evidence.txt`. Without a co-located format definition, the executor and F4's consumer cannot interoperate without an out-of-band agreement.
findings.location: What section — 'Complete the F4 evidence handshake' bullet; AC #4
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sentence such as: 'The `evidence-artifacts` fragment is a plain-text file in GNU sha256sum format (`<64-hex-chars>  <filename>`, two spaces), one line per artifact (wheel, sdist), matching the output of `sha256sum *.whl *.tar.gz`; F4's concatenation step appends it verbatim.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the `test.yml` 'optionally add' scope item: either promote it to in-scope with a corresponding AC (e.g. 'test.yml includes a wheel/sdist probe job that runs on every PR') or explicitly mark it out-of-scope for this story.
findings.finding: The `test.yml` scope bullet says 'optionally add a wheel/sdist artifact probe to regular CI' — this is an unresolved design decision, not a stated default. No acceptance criterion maps to this work, so an executor cannot determine whether to implement it or skip it.
findings.location: Scope section, `.github/workflows/test.yml` bullet
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'optionally add a wheel/sdist artifact probe to regular CI' with either (a) 'Add a wheel/sdist probe step to the existing `test.yml` CI run' and add a matching AC, or (b) 'Out of scope: adding a wheel/sdist probe to test.yml (tracked as follow-on)' — pick one.

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the `pyproject.toml` 'if needed' hedge with a stated default: either 'no pyproject.toml change is expected because `_build_info.py` is generated at build time by the hook and not listed under force-include' or enumerate the specific condition that would trigger a change.
findings.finding: The `pyproject.toml` scope bullet uses 'if needed' to defer a concrete investigation decision to the executor, without stating a default or the conditions under which a change would be required.
findings.location: Scope section, `pyproject.toml` bullet
findings.suggested_fix: Add a parenthetical: '(no change expected — `_build_info.py` is written by the hook at build time and does not require a force-include entry; confirm and skip if so)'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either move the `python -m build` subprocess test to an integration/smoke test suite (e.g. `tests/integration/`) that is guaranteed to run in the release CI environment with the full build toolchain, or add an explicit note in the plan confirming that `tests/unit/test_attestation_signing.py` runs without marker filtering and that `build`/`hatchling` are available as test dependencies.
findings.finding: AC3's proving test runs `python -m build` as a subprocess inside `tests/unit/test_attestation_signing.py`, but unit tests typically run without the full build toolchain available (e.g. in environments filtered by a `unit` pytest marker, in minimal Docker images, or under `--ignore` for slow tests). If the test is excluded from the unit-test suite in practice, the defect (COMMIT=None from sdist→wheel) can silently regress — the plan claims this test 'proves' the fix but places it where it may never run.
findings.location: AC3; Scope section `tests/unit/test_attestation_signing.py` bullet; What section final paragraph
findings.suggested_fix: Place the subprocess-level `python -m build` test in `tests/integration/test_build_provenance.py` (or similar), add it to the `make test` target with the appropriate marker, and update AC3 to reference the integration file. Alternatively, add `build` to the test extras in pyproject.toml and add a plan note confirming no marker filtering applies.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a developer-facing reference for the `REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT` contract — either a doc-comment block at the top of `hatch_build.py` explaining the env var's semantics and fail-fast rule, or an entry in `CLAUDE.md`/`CONTRIBUTING.md` — so the coupling between the workflow and the build hook is discoverable without reading release.yml.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new developer-contract env var (`REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT`) that governs the build hook's behavior, but no update to a developer-facing reference (CLAUDE.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, or an inline contract comment block in `hatch_build.py`) is called for — only `docs/releasing.md` (a user/operator doc) is in scope.
findings.location: Scope — `hatch_build.py` bullet; `docs/releasing.md` bullet; AC item 5
findings.suggested_fix: Add a module-level docstring or comment block to `hatch_build.py` (e.g., 'REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT (env var): when set, used as the authoritative short SHA baked into _build_info.py; must be a non-empty short hex string; unset = git fallback for dev installs; set-but-empty = build failure (release-context fail-fast)') and reference it from releasing.md's Build-once section.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a unit test (in `tests/unit/test_attestation_signing.py` or a dedicated hook test) that sets `REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT=''` (env var present but empty) and asserts the Hatchling hook raises / the build exits non-zero, verifying the fail-fast branch fires as specified.
findings.finding: The fail-fast branch in hatch_build.py (REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT set but empty/falsy → build failure) is specified as new conditional logic but no test for that failure mode is mentioned anywhere in the plan.
findings.location: Scope (hatch_build.py) / AC3 / What ('Fail-fast scoped to the release context')
findings.suggested_fix: In the test added to `tests/unit/test_attestation_signing.py`, alongside the happy-path subprocess test, add a parametrized case: `env = {**os.environ, 'REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT': ''}` → run `python -m build` (or invoke `hatch_build.py`'s `initialize` directly with a mocked env) and assert a non-zero exit / exception is raised. Also add a case where the env var is unset to confirm the git fallback runs without error (graceful degradation).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the canonical artifact action version pair (upload + download) to use for all new and existing jobs in release.yml, and confirm cross-major compatibility or standardize both to the same major version.
findings.finding: The current release.yml already has a version mismatch between upload-artifact@v7 (line 43) and download-artifact@v8 (lines 60, 85). The plan adds new upload/download jobs to release.yml without specifying which action versions to standardize on. Cross-major-version artifact compatibility between upload and download actions is not guaranteed by GitHub — in the v3→v4 split GitHub explicitly broke backward compatibility — so the executor needs a stated policy.
findings.location: Scope → .github/workflows/release.yml; existing release.yml lines 43 (upload-artifact@v7) and 60, 85 (download-artifact@v8)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a note to the Scope bullet for release.yml specifying, e.g., 'standardize all artifact actions to actions/upload-artifact@v4 / actions/download-artifact@v4 (or both at @v8 if that is the chosen pair), resolving the existing @v7/@v8 mismatch.' GitHub's artifact action v4 is the current stable pair with documented cross-job compatibility.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Capture the build-once architectural decision in a persistent maintainer-facing artifact — either a new ADR (e.g., `docs/adr/0001-build-once-release.md`) or a dedicated section in `CLAUDE.md` — summarizing the problem (different build paths, COMMIT=None in sdist→wheel), the chosen pattern, and the invariant that must be preserved (no rebuild between test and publish).
findings.finding: The build-once pipeline pattern (build once → upload → download → hash-verify → publish) is a novel architectural decision for this project, but no ADR or CLAUDE.md update is planned to capture the rationale — future maintainers must reconstruct it from the plan text, which will not persist in the repo.
findings.location: Scope — out-of-scope / architectural pattern; AC items 1–5

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify retry/timeout configuration for all artifact upload and download steps in `release.yml` (e.g., `actions/upload-artifact@v4` with `retention-days` and `if-no-files-found: error`; downstream jobs with explicit `needs:` failure handling), and document the recovery procedure when an artifact upload fails mid-release.
findings.finding: The plan introduces multiple GitHub Actions artifact upload and download operations across the new build, wheel-test, sdist-test, and publish jobs, but specifies no retry, timeout, or error-handling strategy for these steps. GitHub artifact storage is an external I/O dependency that can fail transiently (upload timeouts, download 5xx). A flaky artifact upload in the build job silently blocks all downstream jobs with no recovery path defined.
findings.location: Scope / `.github/workflows/release.yml` — build-once + two artifact-test jobs + hash-gated publish
findings.suggested_fix: Use `actions/upload-artifact@v4` with `if-no-files-found: error` and `retention-days: <N>` on the upload step; add a step-level `continue-on-error: false` and a job-level failure notification. For downloads, use `actions/download-artifact@v4` with explicit error handling. Document: 'if the build job artifact upload fails, re-run only the build job; do not re-run publish independently.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define a namespaced artifact naming convention (e.g., include `${{ github.run_id }}` or the release tag in the artifact name) in `release.yml` to prevent cross-run collisions, specify a `retention-days` value for the artifact upload, and document the expected cleanup behavior.
findings.finding: The plan specifies no RETIRE/cleanup path for the GitHub Actions artifact bundles (the wheel/sdist bundle and the `evidence-artifacts` fragment) that the new build job uploads. Over many releases these accumulate indefinitely against repository storage quotas. Additionally, the plan does not address artifact name collisions when concurrent release runs are in flight (e.g., a hotfix release triggered while a previous release pipeline is still running), which can cause a later build job to overwrite an in-flight artifact that the test/publish jobs of the earlier run are still consuming.
findings.location: Scope / `.github/workflows/release.yml`; Acceptance Criteria AC4 (evidence-artifacts fragment)
findings.suggested_fix: Name the artifact bundle `release-bundle-${{ github.ref_name }}-${{ github.run_id }}` and pass this name through `needs` context to downstream jobs. Set `retention-days: 7` (long enough for the pipeline to complete, short enough to avoid accumulation). Add a post-publish cleanup step or rely on the short retention period.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact behavior of `hatch_build.py`'s `initialize` when `REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT` is unset AND `git rev-parse` also fails: define whether it logs a warning and sets `COMMIT = None`, raises an exception, or sets a sentinel value — and add a unit test covering this branch.
findings.finding: The plan says `hatch_build.py`'s `initialize` will 'fall back to git when [REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT is] unset,' but does not specify the behavior when the env var is unset AND the git fallback also fails (e.g., `git` is not installed, the directory is not a git repo, or `git rev-parse` returns a non-zero exit code). The current code already has this gap (yielding `COMMIT = None`), and the fix description leaves the fallback's error path ambiguous — 'degrades gracefully to the git value' does not define what 'gracefully' means when the git value is also unavailable.
findings.location: `hatch_build.py` — Scope section; 'Fix the hook' subsection of What
findings.suggested_fix: Log a `warnings.warn` at `UserWarning` level ('Could not determine build commit; COMMIT will be None') and set COMMIT=None, preserving backward compatibility for dev installs. Add a test: mock both `os.environ.get` returning None and `subprocess.run` raising FileNotFoundError, assert COMMIT is None and a warning was emitted.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether `test_release_workflow_pins.py` is created by the companion F4 story (and declare the sequencing dependency explicitly) or must be created by this story — the file does not currently exist in the repo.
findings.finding: The plan says to 'extend `test_release_workflow_pins.py` check (f)' as if that test file already exists, but no such file exists anywhere in the repository. A full search finds zero matches for `test_release_workflow_pins` or `workflow_pins` in the `tests/` tree. If the file is being created by a companion story (F4), the plan should say so explicitly; as written it implies extending an existing file, which is a false premise.
findings.location: What section — 'Complete the F4 evidence handshake' bullet; AC line: 'and this story **extends `test_release_workflow_pins.py` check (f)**'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a note in the What section: 'NOTE: `test_release_workflow_pins.py` is created by Finding 4's story; this story's AC 4 work must land after F4, or this story must create the file and F4 extends it.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Remove the contradictory `--no-isolation` dependency claim from the 'Fix the hook' bullet (the 'Verify env-var propagation' bullet already correctly states the actual mechanism — subprocess env inheritance — and correctly says this is not coupled to F4's flag); the 'Fix the hook' bullet's positive rationale should rely on the subprocess semantics explanation, not on a flag owned by another story.
findings.finding: The plan's positive rationale for why REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT propagates into the Hatchling hook during `python -m build`'s sdist→wheel sub-build is mechanically wrong: it says 'guaranteed because Finding 4 builds with `--no-isolation`', but pypa/build invokes the Hatchling backend via subprocess.run that inherits the parent process environment regardless of PEP 517 build-dependency isolation — --no-isolation is not needed for env-var propagation. This incorrect stated rationale may cause the implementer to (a) believe this story blocks on Finding 4's --no-isolation flag, or (b) add --no-isolation to this story's build invocation as a workaround, creating an unintended coupling.
findings.location: What section — 'Verify env-var propagation' bullet: 'Env vars propagate to the build backend subprocess via inherited os.environ regardless of PEP 517 isolation — isolation governs build *dependencies*, not the environment — so this is not coupled to F4's `--no-isolation` choice'
findings.suggested_fix: In the 'Fix the hook' bullet, replace 'On the release path this is guaranteed because Finding 4 builds with `--no-isolation`' with: 'pypa/build calls the Hatchling backend via a subprocess that inherits the parent process environment, so REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT propagates through the sdist→wheel sub-build regardless of PEP 517 build-dependency isolation — no --no-isolation flag is needed for this.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Ground the env-var propagation capability assertion by citing the specific pypa/build source (or PEP 517 spec section) that confirms the build backend subprocess inherits the parent's `os.environ`, rather than relying solely on the proof-test obligation to discover this at implementation time.
findings.finding: The plan asserts as settled fact that REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT propagates from the workflow shell through pypa/build's PEP 517 backend subprocess into the Hatchling initialize() hook during the sdist→wheel sub-build ('Env vars propagate to the build backend subprocess via inherited os.environ regardless of PEP 517 isolation'), but provides no code citation, PEP reference, or pypa/build source inspection to ground this claim. The proof-test mitigates at implementation time but the plan treats an unverified capability assertion as established fact — if wrong, the entire core fix mechanism is invalid.
findings.location: What section — 'Verify env-var propagation (do not assume)' bullet
findings.suggested_fix: Add a one-sentence citation to the plan: pypa/build's `_runner.py` calls `subprocess.run([sys.executable, ...], env=os.environ)` (or equivalent) when invoking the build backend, meaning inherited env vars reach Hatchling's hook. Reference the pypa/build source file + line, or the relevant PEP 517 section stating isolation is dependency-only. If this cannot be cited, keep the proof-test but remove the 'this is settled fact' parenthetical and leave the behavior explicitly unverified-until-test.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in AC #4 and the What section whether `test_release_workflow_pins.py` must be **created** by this story (specifying its full check-set) or is a **pre-existing deliverable from F4** that this story extends — resolve the pink-elephant reference to a non-existent file.
findings.finding: The plan references 'test_release_workflow_pins.py check (f)' as an existing artifact to extend (both in the What section and AC #4), but this file does not exist anywhere in the repository. The executor would treat 'extend check (f)' as an instruction to modify an existing file, but must instead create it (or discover it belongs entirely to the concurrent F4 story). This is an instruction-locality / pink-elephant antipattern.
findings.location: What section — 'Complete the F4 evidence handshake' bullet; AC #4
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'extends `test_release_workflow_pins.py` check (f)' with 'creates `tests/unit/test_release_workflow_pins.py` (if not already delivered by F4) containing at minimum check (f): assert that the `evidence-artifacts` artifact name and its upload/download wiring appear in `release.yml`'; add a story-dependency note that if F4 delivers the file first, this story adds to it.

findings.finding: AC3's proving test is placed in `tests/unit/test_attestation_signing.py` but the plan does not specify whether the test invokes `python -m build` end-to-end (the production sdist→wheel subprocess path) or only exercises the Hatchling hook's env-var reading in isolation — these two test shapes prove entirely different things, and the plan's own text says the test must 'run REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT=<short-hex> python -m build and assert the resulting wheel carries that exact short SHA'.
findings.location: AC3; What section ('Verify env-var propagation'); Scope — `tests/unit/test_attestation_signing.py`
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC3: 'The test invokes `subprocess.run(["python", "-m", "build", "--no-isolation"], env={**os.environ, "REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT": "abc1234"}, ...)`, extracts the built wheel with `zipfile`, and asserts `_build_info.COMMIT == "abc1234"`. This test may live in `tests/integration/` to separate it from fast unit tests.'

findings.finding: AC3 says 'a test asserts the wheel built via the default sdist→wheel path contains COMMIT equal to that SHA' and assigns it to `tests/unit/test_attestation_signing.py`, but the body explicitly warns 'rather than asserting propagation as settled fact' and demands a test that 'runs `REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT=<40-char-hex> python -m build`'. A unit test in that file exercising a direct `WheelBuilder` call (the current pattern in lines 202-227) can satisfy the AC letter without ever invoking `python -m build` end-to-end, leaving the sdist→wheel subprocess path untested — exactly the defect the plan aims to close.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3; What section — 'Verify env-var propagation' bullet; `tests/unit/test_attestation_signing.py`
findings.suggested_fix: Rewrite the AC3 proving clause: 'A test (may live in `tests/integration/` or as a separate fixture) invokes `REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT=<40-char-hex> python -m build --no-isolation --outdir <tmpdir>` in a temp directory, unpacks the resulting `.whl`, imports `_build_info`, and asserts `COMMIT == <sha>`; a direct WheelBuilder call does NOT satisfy this criterion.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
